sourceMovie.title: Australia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Australia works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Far and Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Australia clicked for you, Far and Away (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Australia?
faq.answer: Try Far and Away, The African Queen, The Curious Case of Benjamin Button—each shares DNA with Australia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Australia?
faq.answer: Australia (2008) is best described as Adventure, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Australia worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
